description: When we're composing an answer, the question should be fixed. It came from a specific question. Also, the only thing we should select the side, AI will figure that out. We should just type our answer or speak our answer and also remove the token from down below. We'll handle that server side. So the answer should literally just be a fixed question. The answer sheet is a fixed question and then a text box and recording button. And then they can choose to either use one of those to submit their answer.

results.summary: Trimmed the answer sheet to a fixed question, a text box, and a recording button
results.identified_issue: Description asked to (1) show the question as fixed rather than a picker since the composer is always opened on a specific question from Discover, (2) drop side selection because the AI classifies the side, and (3) remove the OpenRouter token UI ('we'll handle that server side'). I set claimedSide to nil on both the analysis call and the saved Answer; AnswerAnalysisService already treats claimedSide as optional and the AI/heuristic derives leanSide, and resolvedSide falls back to leanSide, so deck ordering still works. The token entry point is gone from this screen but AnswerAnalysisService still reads the token from Keychain if one exists — nothing server-side was wired up here, so with no token the on-device heuristic is what runs. If the intent was a real server-side analysis endpoint, that's a follow-up.
results.detailed_description: I rewrote SubmitAnswerView so the composer is exactly what you described: the question it was opened on is shown as fixed headline text (no more question Picker), followed by the answer text box and the on-device recording/dictation button, and a Submit button. I removed the 'Your claimed side' segmented control — the AI now determines which side the answer lands on during analysis (claimedSide is passed as nil, and the service already derives leanSide from the text). I also removed the 'Add OpenRouter token' section and its sheet from the bottom of the screen. One thing to flag: the token was the only thing making analysis call a real model; with it gone from the UI, analysis falls back to the on-device heuristic unless a token is already stored. You mentioned handling that server side — that server endpoint isn't built yet, so if you want real AI classification we'll need to wire the app to your backend next. I did not build (vigil handles that on push).
